items.title: Some Americans Aren't Looking to Save on Household Goods—They're 'Trading Up'
items.description: Consumers are buying more high-end toothpaste, hair care products and other staples, and less of the bargain alternatives, Procter & Gamble and Colgate-Palmolive said.

items.title: Tylenol trust crisis: Trump, RFK Jr. and Texas lawsuits slam Kenvue
items.description: Kenvue, the company that makes Tylenol, is fighting on both the political and scientific fronts. The latest blow came from the U.S. Health Secretary, placing even more pressure on Kenvue stock, which has already lost 40% of its value in the last six months. Robert F. Kennedy Jr. recently made ...

items.title: Kimberly-Clark Buying Tylenol Maker Kenvue in $40 Billion Deal
items.description: Kimberly-Clark agreed to buy Kenvue in a deal worth roughly $40 billion in a combination that could create a company with $32 billion in revenue and allow it to surpass Unilever to become the second-biggest seller of health and wellness products after Procter & Gamble. Eric Pfanner reports on Bloomberg Television.

items.title: Kimberly-Clark Strikes $40 Billion Deal for Tylenol Maker Kenvue
items.description: One of biggest takeovers so far this year will create a global company with annual revenues of roughly $32 billion.

items.title: Merck, Nike share losses contribute to Dow's 134-point drop
